content.plain_english: This week in EYFS, your child is exploring Consolidation. They will focus on: match and sort; compare amounts; compare size, mass and capacity. All of this happens through play, stories, and practical activities that build early mathematical curiosity and confidence.
content.in_school: In sessions, children explore Consolidation through play, stories, and practical activities using real objects. Their teacher guides them through careful conversation, helping them notice patterns and relationships without formal recording.
content.home_activity: Spend a few minutes exploring consolidation together at home. Use whatever is around — pasta shapes to sort, buttons to count, or water to pour into different containers. Ask your child to show you what they have been doing in school. There are no wrong answers — just curious conversations and playful exploration.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me something at home that is about consolidation?
What was your favourite thing you did in maths today?
Can you teach me one thing you learned this week?

key_vocabulary.definition: Look back over what you have learned to check and strengthen understanding.
Use your knowledge in a new or different situation.
Explain why an answer is correct using mathematical language.
Working through a challenge by choosing and using the right maths skills.
A planned method used to approach and solve a problem.

example_questions: Can you count to 10 and back to 0?
Show me something heavier than this book.
Can you find two things that are the same shape?
content.times_table_tip: At this stage children are not learning times tables formally. Instead they explore equal groups through play — sharing objects fairly or making equal piles — which builds the foundation for multiplication later on.
